examples.title: Basic Inline Function Example
examples.description: Demonstrates declaration and usage of inline functions
examples.explanation: The inline keyword suggests compiler to replace function calls with the function body. For small functions like square and max, this eliminates call overhead.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) - direct execution, no call overhead

examples.title: Inline vs Normal Function - Performance Comparison
examples.description: Shows when compiler may or may not inline functions
examples.explanation: The compiler decides whether to actually inline based on function complexity. Small functions like add() are likely inlined, while large or recursive functions may not be.
examples.spaceComplexity: Code bloat possible for large inlined functions

examples.title: Inline Member Functions in Class
examples.description: Demonstrates implicit and explicit inline for class member functions
examples.explanation: Member functions defined inside class are automatically inline. Functions defined outside can use explicit 'inline' keyword. Both approaches create inline functions.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) where n = number of call sites (code duplication)
